evals.prompt: Write HTML for an email signup form with a visible label, required indicator, and submit button.
evals.expected_output: Uses a labeled email input with type="email", marks the field required in text, and does not rely on placeholder text as the only label.
expectations: The email field has a visible label
The email input uses type="email"
Required status is communicated in text
Placeholder text is not the only label

evals.prompt: Create accessible validation markup for a form field with an error message after a failed submit.
evals.expected_output: Marks the field aria-invalid="true", ties the error message to the field with aria-describedby, and uses clear actionable error text.
expectations: The invalid field is marked aria-invalid="true"
The error message is referenced by aria-describedby
The error copy tells the user how to fix the problem
The error is not communicated by color alone

evals.prompt: Write a radio button group for preferred contact method.
evals.expected_output: Uses a fieldset and legend for the group, with each radio control labelled and grouped semantically.
expectations: Related radio controls are wrapped in a fieldset
The group has a legend
Each radio option has a label
The markup is understandable without CSS

evals.prompt: Build a date of birth form that works well without a custom date picker.
evals.expected_output: Uses three separate labelled inputs for day, month, and year with sensible autocomplete hints, rather than a custom calendar widget.
expectations: The date is split into day, month, and year inputs
Each input has its own label
The inputs use appropriate autocomplete values
The solution avoids a custom calendar widget

evals.prompt: Write markup for a quantity field where the user should enter a numeric code, not a spinner control.
evals.expected_output: Uses type="text" with inputmode="numeric" and a pattern, instead of type="number".
expectations: The input uses text rather than number
The numeric keyboard hint is provided with inputmode="numeric"
A pattern is supplied for numeric entry
The solution avoids unwanted spinner behaviour
